summary: The process-exit callback bridge at 0x1407D81B4 first handles the encoded single callback slot 0x140C56A08, then drains one of two encoded dynamic on-exit table contexts, then walks two static callback ranges.
The static callback walker at 0x1407D94BC walks qwords from RCX up to RDX, skips null entries, and guard-dispatches each non-null callback through the CFG dispatch pointer at 0x140803068.

The first static range 0x140805020..0x140805040 contains a null slot followed by callbacks 0x1407D9A0C, 0x1407EC640, and 0x1407C59C4. The second static range 0x140805048..0x140805050 is currently a single null slot.

The dynamic contexts at 0x140C56B48 and 0x140C56B60 are virtual .data triads encoded with security cookie 0x140C361A8. Each triad is begin/current/end, with each pointer XORed and rotated by the cookie-derived shift.
0x1407D9184 drains a selected dynamic context under critical-section index 2 by calling 0x1407D8E68, which enters the indexed lock, calls 0x1407D9054, then leaves the same lock.

0x1407D9054 decodes begin/current, walks callback slots backward from current-8 to begin, replaces each slot with the cookie sentinel before guard-dispatching the decoded callback, then frees the vector and resets begin/current/end to the cookie sentinel.
0x1407D8EA4 appends a callback into a dynamic context, growing the vector through 0x1407E6BE0 when current reaches end and filling newly reserved slots with the cookie sentinel via REP STOSQ.
0x1407D924C resets both dynamic contexts with 0x1407D91C8, but only when a context is empty enough for begin to equal end.

dynamic_tables.selected_when: 0x1407D81D8 loads state 0 from the exit bridge argument
0x1407D821C sees state 1 from the exit bridge argument

tooling_notes: pe-instructions now decodes F3 48 AB as REP STOSQ so callback-vector reserve/fill loops retain their semantic label in generated artifacts.
pe-read-qwords confirmed the static callback slots and import hint/name records; virtual .data contexts intentionally report as virtual-only because they are runtime-initialized.
next_steps: Follow static callbacks 0x1407D9A0C, 0x1407EC640, and 0x1407C59C4 to name their teardown responsibilities.
Find callers of 0x1407D9174 and 0x1407D91F0 to identify who registers dynamic on-exit callbacks.
Map the state object passed into 0x1407D81B4 so the state 0/state 1 dynamic-table choice can be named.
